column1: # glyph-map.tsv
# Deterministic post-extraction repair table for vault-source-ingest.
# Format: two tab-separated columns, `from<TAB>to`. Plain string substitution
# only — no regex, no LaTeX wrapping, no inference. Applied in file order;
# each row is a literal find/replace over the raw tool output.
# This file must be byte-identical across machines. It is the canonical
# artifact (a - logistics/Source Note Types.md, "What exactly gets frozen"):
# the frozen <source name>.extract.txt is this map applied to the raw tool
# output, so a substitution here changes what future quote-matching checks
# against. A row removed or edited does not retroactively fix an already-
# frozen .extract.txt — that takes a re-extraction, producing a new dated file.
# What belongs here: 1:1 character/sequence substitutions that are correct
# regardless of source PDF — standard ligature codepoints decomposed to their
# ASCII letter sequence, and specific tool-glyph-name artifacts confirmed by
# inspecting real extractor output (not guessed in advance).
# What does NOT belong here: letter-spacing collapse ("T w o" -> "Two"). There
# is no lookup table for that — single letters separated by spaces are
# everywhere in stat-mech notation (n k T, loose indices), so a rejoin rule
# corrupts legitimate physics as often as it fixes garbled prose. That failure
# mode is detected and flagged, never repaired; see the standard.
# --- Standard Unicode ligature decompositions (tool-agnostic) ---
ﬁ
ﬂ
